page: Microsoft Azure E32-16ads_v7 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the Eadsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with 8 physical cores, operating at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 16.0 GB per core. Local storage is bundled with a capacity of 1890 GB, and the instance is Premium Storage capable. No GPU accelerators are present on this server type.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier single-core performance. The stress-ng single-core CPU test and single-threaded gzip compression both rank in the top 10%, alongside single-threaded bzip3 decompression. However, multi-core and multi-threaded performance metrics, such as the stress-ng multi-core CPU test and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, fall within the average range. Memory bandwidth tests for both small and large blocks, as well as PassMark CPU and memory scores, also align with average tier performance. For language model workloads, the instance achieves top-tier text generation speeds for small 135M models, but performance drops to the bottom 10% for prompt processing on large 70B models.

From a resource density perspective, this instance offers high memory capacity relative to its vCPU count, making it qualitatively cost-effective for memory-bound workloads that do not require massive parallel compute scaling. The primary tradeoff lies in the contrast between its top-tier single-core execution speed and its average multi-core scaling. The presence of 1890 GB of local storage combined with 256.0 GB of RAM makes this configuration suitable for memory-intensive applications, database operations, caching layers, and small-scale language model inference.
description: Microsoft Azure E32-16ads_v7 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server instance from the Eadsv7 family, running on Microsoft Hyper-V. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs from an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with 8 physical cores running at 4.5 GHz, paired with 256.0 GB of RAM and 1890 GB of local storage. The instance demonstrates top-tier single-core CPU performance and single-threaded compression speeds, though its multi-core scaling and memory bandwidth align with average benchmarks. With a high memory-to-core ratio of 16.0 GB per core and premium storage capabilities, it represents a balanced option for memory-intensive workloads. It is well-suited for database operations, caching layers, and small-scale language model inference where single-threaded execution speed is prioritized over massive multi-core parallelization.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E32-16ads_v7 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server featuring 16 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 256 GB of RAM, and 1890 GB of local storage, delivering top-tier single-core performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E32-16ads_v7 is a memory-optimized server with 16 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 1890 GB local storage for database workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized x86_64 server featuring high single-core performance, substantial system memory, and integrated local storage for data-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with 16 dedicated vCPUs running at 4.5 GHz
256.0 GB of system memory providing a 16:1 memory-to-vCPU ratio
1890 GB of bundled local storage with Premium Storage capability
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
Average multi-core scaling and memory bandwidth benchmarks
Suitable for database operations, caching, and small-scale LLM inference
